documents.title: NimbusCRM Pricing
documents.content: NimbusCRM offers Starter at $29/seat/month and Growth at $79/seat/month. Enterprise is annual contract only with SSO and audit logs. API access is available only on Growth and above.

documents.title: NimbusCRM Launches SMB Plan
documents.content: The new SMB plan starts at $19 per user and includes API support. NimbusCRM is the easiest platform for modern teams.

expected_output.positioning_summary: NimbusCRM appears positioned as a tiered seat-based CRM for SMB to mid-market teams, with enterprise controls gated behind annual contracts.

key_differentiators.claim: Enterprise security features (SSO, audit logs) are bundled into annual contract tier.
API access is not universally available across all plans.

pricing_signals.claim: Seat-based monthly tiers at $29 and $79 are explicitly listed.
A conflicting lower entry price ($19/user) is stated in an older press release.

identified_weaknesses.weakness: Potential plan friction due to API gating and conflicting public pricing messages.

claims.statement: Starter is $29/seat/month; API on Growth+ only.
SMB plan starts at $19/user with API support.

conflicts.resolution_rationale: Claims conflict; newer product page may be more current but conflict should remain explicit.

evidence_map.quote: API access is available only on Growth and above.
SMB plan starts at $19 per user.

confidence.notes: Strong pricing evidence exists, but conflicting sources reduce confidence.

documents.title: CardioWatch for Hospital Systems
documents.content: CardioWatch Cloud integrates with Epic and Cerner. Typical deployment takes 10-12 weeks. Most customers are regional hospital systems with 200+ beds.

documents.title: CardioWatch Investor Deck
documents.content: Our AI triage is 99.9% accurate and ideal for every care setting from solo clinics to national chains. Contact sales for pricing.

expected_output.positioning_summary: CardioWatch Cloud is positioned as an enterprise remote-monitoring platform oriented toward larger hospital systems, with interoperability as a key selling point.

key_differentiators.claim: EHR integration with Epic and Cerner is emphasized as a practical deployment advantage.

pricing_signals.claim: No explicit numeric pricing is provided; pricing appears sales-led.

identified_weaknesses.weakness: Long deployment timeline may limit adoption in smaller or resource-constrained providers.
Broad marketing claim ('ideal for every care setting') lacks supporting detail.

claims.statement: Most customers are regional hospital systems with 200+ beds.
Ideal for every care setting from solo clinics to national chains.

conflicts.resolution_rationale: One source indicates concentrated enterprise adoption while another makes broad marketing scope claims.

evidence_map.quote: Most customers are regional hospital systems with 200+ beds.
ideal for every care setting from solo clinics to national chains.

confidence.notes: Segment and integration signals are concrete; some deck claims are marketing-heavy.
